Configure which paths to scan for unused: Configure which paths to scan for unused datapoints. Use the object picker or type a path manually. Each path will get its own result state with a JSON table.
Datapoints listed on the Exceptions tab:
Datapoints listed on the Exceptions tab are omitted from all scan results and statistics. Use this for configuration values that rarely change but should not appear as dead or stale.

Use the picker to select individual datapoints (states). To exclude an entire folder or subtree, type the path directly into the field (e.g. 0_userdata.0.config).

Each scan channel provides exceptionCount showing how many datapoints were excluded during the last scan (0 if none).
Datapoints not updated for this many day: Datapoints not updated for this many days are marked as 'dead'
Datapoints not updated for this many days are marked as 'stale'
Each scan result contains a JSON array w: Each scan result contains a JSON array with the following 10 fields per datapoint:
Exclude datapoints from scan results tha: Exclude datapoints from scan results that are intentionally static or should be ignored. Use the picker to select individual datapoints. To exclude an entire folder, type the path manually (e.g. 0_userdata.0.config).
For German tables: Use status_de and iss:
For German tables: Use status_de and issue_de columns.
For English tables: Use status and issue columns

Filter by 'issue != null' to show only problematic datapoints.
For alias.* paths: Check if target datap: For alias.* paths: Check if target datapoints exist (ghost detection)
How often the automatic scan should run: How often the automatic scan should run (minimum 1 hour)
If enabled, the adapter will scan the en: If enabled, the adapter will scan the entire object tree and store the result in a separate 'complete' result channel.
Optional note, e.g. alarm clock radio st: Optional note, e.g. alarm clock radio station
Optional. If empty, the path will be use: Optional. If empty, the path will be used as name.
Pick a namespace (e.g. 0_userdata.0, ali: Pick a namespace (e.g. 0_userdata.0, alias.0). The path stays editable — remove the instance number (alias.0 → alias) to scan all instances.
Pick an individual datapoint to exclude: Pick an individual datapoint to exclude it. To exclude an entire folder, type the folder path directly (e.g. 0_userdata.0.config).
Tip: The path stays editable after picki: Tip: The path stays editable after picking. Remove the instance number (e.g. alias.0 → alias) to scan all instances. You can also copy any object ID from the Objects tab and paste it directly into the path field.
Use the object picker on the Scan Paths:
Use the object picker on the Scan Paths tab or type a path manually. The path stays editable after picking.

To scan all instances of an adapter, remove the instance number from the path (e.g. alias.0 → alias). This matches alias.0, alias.1, and so on.
When enabled, all configured paths will: When enabled, all configured paths will be scanned automatically at the specified interval
